name: FuzzyFloat
javadoc:
/** * Routines for doing approximate ('fuzzy') floating point comparisons. * Those are comparisons that only require the floating point numbers to be * relatively close to one another to be equal, rather than exactly * equal.

* * Floating point numbers are compared for equality by dividing them by * one another and comparing the ratio. By default they must be within * 0.0001 (0.01%) to be considered equal; supply this value to the * FuzzyFloat constructor, or set the value with the set_rel_diff method. * Note that zero is never equal to a non-zero number using this method.

* * Two NaN floats are not considered equal (consistent with the == operator). */

members.name: FuzzyFloat
FuzzyFloat
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Creates a FuzzyFloat.
 * Specify the specific relative difference allowed between two
 * floats in order for them to be equal.  The default is 0.0001
 * a relative diff of zero, disables it (i.e., only exact matches work).
 * @param rel_diff the relative diff to use
 */

members.name: set_rel_diff
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Set all the fields of this class.
 * @param rel_diff the new relative diff to use
 * @see #FuzzyFloat
 */

tags.name: rel_diff
tags.content: the new relative diff to use
tags.description: the new relative diff to use

members.name: eq
members.javadoc:
/** * Test d1 and d2 for equality using the current ratio. Two NaN floats * are not considered equal (consistent with the == operator).

* * Note that if one of the numbers if 0.0, then the other number must * be less than the square of the fuzzy ratio. This policy accommodates * round off errors in floating point values. * * @param d1 the first value to compare * @param d2 the first value to compare * @return true if d1 and d2 are considered equal, false otherwise */

tags.content: if d1 and d2 are considered equal, false otherwise
tags.assertion: assert methodResult == (args[0] == args[1] || Math.abs(args[0] - args[1]) < Math.pow(getFuzzyRatio(), 2));
tags.description: true if d1 and d2 are considered equal, false otherwise

members.name: ne
members.javadoc:
/**
   * Test d1 and d2 for non-equality using the current ratio.
   * @param d1 the first value to compare
   * @param d2 the first value to compare
   * @return whether d1 and d2 are non-equal
   * @see #eq
   */

tags.description: whether d1 and d2 are non-equal

members.name: lt
members.javadoc:
/**
   * Test d1 and d2 for d1 < d2.  If d1 is equal to d2 using the current ratio
   * this returns false.
   * @param d1 the first value to compare
   * @param d2 the first value to compare
   * @return whether d1 < d2
   * @see #eq
   */

members.name: lte
members.javadoc:
/**
   * test d1 and  d2 for d1 ≤ d2.  If d1 is equal to d2 using the current
   * ratio, this returns true.
   * @param d1 the first value to compare
   * @param d2 the first value to compare
   * @return whether d1 ≤ d2
   * @see #eq
   */

members.name: gt
members.javadoc:
/**
   * test d1 and d2  for d1 > d2.  IF d1 is equal to d2 using the current
   * ratio, this returns false.
   * @param d1 the first value to compare
   * @param d2 the first value to compare
   * @return whether d1 > d2
   * @see #eq
   */

members.javadoc:
/**

   * ratio, this returns true.
   * @param d1 the first value to compare
   * @param d2 the first value to compare
   * @return whether d1 > d2
   * @see #eq

members.name: indexOf
members.javadoc:
/**
   * Searches for the first occurrence of elt in a.  elt is considered
   * equal to a[i] if it passes the {@link #eq} test.
   *
   * @param a the array to search
   * @param elt the element to search for
   * @return the first index containing the specified element,
   *    or -1 if the element is not found in the array
   * @see java.util.Vector#indexOf(java.lang.Object)
   */

tags.content: first index containing the specified element, or -1 if the element is not found in the array
tags.description: the first index containing the specified element, or -1 if the element is not found in the array

members.name: indexOf
members.javadoc:
/**
   * Searches for the first subsequence of a that matches sub elementwise.
   * Elements of sub are considered to match elements of a if they pass
   * the {@link #eq} test.
   *
   * @param a the sequence to search in
   * @param sub the sequence to search for
   * @return the first index whose subarray is equal to the specified array
   *    or -1 if no such subarray is found in the array
   * @see java.util.Vector#indexOf(java.lang.Object)
   * @see java.lang.String#indexOf(java.lang.String)
   */

tags.content: first index whose subarray is equal to the specified array or -1 if no such subarray is found in the array
tags.description: the first index whose subarray is equal to the specified array or -1 if no such subarray is found in the array

members.name: isElemMatch
members.javadoc:
/** * Determines whether or not a1 and a2 are set equivalent (contain only the * same elements). Element comparison uses {@link #eq}.

* * Note that this implementation is optimized for cases where the * elements are actually the same, since it does a sort of both arrays * before starting the comparisons. * * @param a1 the first value to compare * @param a2 the first value to compare * @return true if a1 and a2 are set equivalent, false otherwise */

tags.content: if a1 and a2 are set equivalent, false otherwise
tags.description: true if a1 and a2 are set equivalent, false otherwise

members.name: isSubset
members.javadoc:
/** * Determines whether smaller is a subset of bigger. Element * comparison uses {@link #eq}.

* * Note that this implementation is optimized for cases where the * elements are actually the same, since it does a sort of both * arrays before starting the comparisons. * * @param smaller the possibly-smaller subset * @param bigger the possibly-larger set * @return true if smaller is a subset (each element of smaller is * also a element of bigger) of bigger, false otherwise */

tags.content: if smaller is a subset (each element of smaller is also a element of bigger) of bigger, false otherwise
tags.description: true if smaller is a subset (each element of smaller is also a element of bigger) of bigger, false otherwise

name: DoubleArrayComparatorLexical

members.name: compare
members.javadoc:
/**
     * Lexically compares o1 and o2 as double arrays.
     *
     * @param a1 the first array to compare
     * @param a2 the second array to compare
     * @return positive if o1 > 02, 0 if o1 == o2, negative if o1 < o2
     */

tags.content: if o1 > 02, 0 if o1 == o2, negative if o1 < o2
